The dividend “will not be quoted ex” means that the company’s dividend will not be quoted on the ex-dividend date. This is usually done to prevent the stock from being overpriced and to give investors a fair price to buy the stock.

The ex-dividend date is the date on which the stock market stops trading shares for a dividend. The ex-dividend date is usually set by the company’s board of directors and is published in the company’s annual report.

judged_items.prompt: How do our lungs filter out dust and other particles in the air ? So I ’ve been working in an old warehouse which is covered in dust .. I have n’t ingested much but I ’m curious how / if it exits our lungs ? Explain like I'm five.

judged_items.text:
How do we filter out dust and other particles in the air?
You know how we breathe air in and out of our lungs? Well, our lungs have special filters called cilia. These little hairs help to trap dust and other particles in the air, so they don't get into our lungs. It's like when you try to pick up sand with a big spoon - the cilia do the same thing, but with much smaller particles. So, when you breathe in dust from your warehouse, your cilia help to keep it from getting into your lungs.

judged_items.prompt: Please explain what is "Word embedding"
judged_items.label: pair_67
judged_items.text: Word embedding is a vector space representation of words in which words that are similar in meaning are closer to each other in space. This representation is usually achieved by learning a low-dimensional space (often 2-4 dimensional) from huge amounts of text. This is done by training a neural network (or some other machine learning algorithm) to map words to points in the space so that words that are close together in the space (and are therefore similar in meaning) are more likely to be in the same context (i.e. to be followed by the same words). The goal of word embedding is to learn a mapping from words to vectors that will help us better understand what words mean, how they are related, and how to use them in context.

judged_items.prompt: Please explain what is "Mechanism design"
judged_items.label: pair_46
judged_items.text: Mechanism design is a subfield of economics that focuses on designing and analyzing incentive-compatible mechanisms or games for achieving a desired outcome. It involves creating rules, structures, and incentives that lead to efficient and optimal results, even when the involved parties have self-interested preferences. Mechanism design is important in fields such as game theory, microeconomics, and social choice theory, where it is used to address problems such as auctions, voting systems, and public good provision.

judged_items.prompt: Please explain what is "James Manyika"
judged_items.label: pair_32
judged_items.text:
James Manyika is a renowned economist and author who is currently the McKinsey Global Institute's (MGI) chairman and a director at McKinsey & Company. Manyika is known for his expertise in economics, finance, and management, and he has written extensively on a wide range of topics, including globalization, innovation, and technology.

Manyika's work at McKinsey & Company has been widely respected, and he has been a sought-after expert on global issues and trends. He is also the co-author of the McKinsey Global Institute's (MGI) "The Future of Work" report, which has had a significant impact on the way that people think about work and the future of the economy.

judged_items.text:
So , you know how your computer is on a network right ? You can send emails , play games , and do other stuff on the internet . The internet is sort of like a big network of networks . There are millions of computers connected to it , all talking to each other .

Now , the big network is run by a bunch of people who work for a company called the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers ( ICANN ) . They make sure that everyone uses the same names for things like websites , and that computers can talk to each other .

judged_items.prompt: Why does water has to boil before I may put the noodles in ? Why is it not enough to be just hot ? Does something special happen to the food I put in the water if it starts boiling ? Explain like I'm five.
judged_items.label: pair_30
judged_items.text: Noodles are not the only food you can cook in boiling water. You can cook rice, potatoes, beans, vegetables, and many other things that way. You need to boil water before you can cook anything in it because water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit) and at that temperature, the water starts to turn into steam. If you tried to cook your noodles at a lower temperature, they would just get hot, but they wouldn't be cooked. Boiling water is like a big, open fire that cooks your food nicely.
